relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Tax collectors and misaligned ones draw near to hear.
Religious grumbling objects to receiving and eating with them.
Jesus answers grumbling with parable.
The shepherd seeks the one lost sheep until found.
Finding becomes shoulder-carried joy.
The house is called into rejoicing.
Heaven rejoices over one turning heart.
The woman lights, sweeps, and seeks the lost coin carefully.
Finding calls neighbors into joy.
The messengers of the Divine rejoice over one turning.
The father has two sons.
The younger asks for inheritance as though already separate.
Distance and waste scatter the living.
Famine reveals need after everything is spent.
He joins himself to a foreign field among pigs.
Hunger falls below the pigs’ food.
Coming to himself begins the return.
He rises with confession toward the father.
He rehearses unworthiness and hired-worker status.
The father sees him far off, runs, embraces, and kisses.
The son speaks confession.
The father answers with robe, ring, and sandals.
The feast begins.
What seemed gone returns to life; what was lost is found.
The older son approaches from the field and hears celebration.
He asks what is happening.
The brother has returned whole.
Anger keeps him outside, and the father comes out to him too.
Service without joy becomes resentment.
He refuses brother-language and names the other as this son of yours.
The father answers: child, all mine is yours.
Joy is necessary because this brother has returned from loss into life.
